dimensions.name: coverage_and_structure
dimensions.description: Does the paragraph cover key themes from the provided papers? Is there a logical structure (e.g., chronological, thematic)?

anchors.1: Misses most papers or presents a disorganized list
anchors.3: Covers most papers with partial synthesis
anchors.5: Synthesizes all key themes into a coherent narrative arc

dimensions.name: faithfulness
dimensions.description: Are all citations grounded in the provided abstracts? No fabricated authors, titles, or findings.

anchors.1: Contains fabricated citations or findings
anchors.5: Fully faithful to source material

dimensions.name: synthesis_quality
dimensions.description: Does the paragraph synthesize across papers rather than summarize each individually?

anchors.1: Paper-by-paper list with no integration
anchors.5: Full synthesis: papers are organized by theme/debate with explicit connections

dimensions.name: academic_tone
dimensions.description: Is the language appropriate for an academic paper?

anchors.1: Informal, colloquial, or promotional language
anchors.3: Mostly academic with occasional informal phrases
anchors.5: Fully academic: precise, hedged where appropriate, no colloquialisms

hard_deductions.condition: Any fabricated citation (author/title not in input)
Directly contradicts a finding stated in an abstract

dimensions.name: numerical_accuracy
dimensions.description: Are all reported coefficients, standard errors, p-values, and statistics copied correctly?

anchors.1: Multiple numerical errors
anchors.5: All numbers perfectly accurate

dimensions.name: economic_interpretation
dimensions.description: Does the text correctly interpret the economic meaning (not just the statistical significance) of the results?

anchors.1: Only restates numbers, no economic meaning
anchors.5: Full economic interpretation with context and units

dimensions.name: causal_language
dimensions.description: Is causal language used appropriately given the design (OLS vs. IV vs. DiD)?

anchors.1: Unwarranted causal claims (e.g., OLS called causal without discussion)
anchors.5: Precisely calibrated: OLS described as 'association', IV/DiD described as 'causal effect'

dimensions.name: statistical_language
dimensions.description: Is statistical significance described correctly (p-values, confidence intervals, not 'proves')?

anchors.1: Says 'proves' or 'confirms' without qualification
anchors.5: Correct significance language with levels and standard errors reported

hard_deductions.condition: Omits reporting first-stage F-stat when design is IV
States OLS result is causal without discussion

dimensions.name: problem_importance
dimensions.description: Does the paragraph convince the reader that the research question matters?

anchors.1: Research question importance is not established
anchors.3: Importance is asserted but not supported with evidence
anchors.5: Importance is compellingly demonstrated with facts, stakes, and context

dimensions.name: literature_gap
dimensions.description: Is the gap in existing literature clearly and accurately identified?

anchors.1: No gap identified
anchors.5: Specific, defensible gap identified with reference to what existing work has and has not done

dimensions.name: hook_quality
dimensions.description: Does the opening engage the reader and motivate continued reading?

anchors.1: Opens with a generic statement (e.g., 'X is an important topic')
anchors.5: Compelling hook: a striking fact, puzzle, or contradiction that creates tension

dimensions.name: research_objective_clarity
dimensions.description: Is the research objective (what this paper does) stated clearly and positioned against the gap?

anchors.1: Research objective is absent or vague
anchors.3: Objective stated but not connected to the gap
anchors.5: Objective directly addresses the identified gap, with method previewed

hard_deductions.condition: Opens with 'X is very important' without evidence
Research objective contradicts the stated gap

dimensions.name: language_quality
dimensions.description: Is the English idiomatic, precise, and free of colloquialisms or Chinese-English interference?

anchors.1: Frequent unidiomatic phrases or grammatical errors
anchors.5: Fully idiomatic, publication-ready English

dimensions.name: structure
dimensions.description: Does the abstract follow the standard structure: motivation/background → method → results → implication?

anchors.1: Structure is absent or random
anchors.3: Most components present but not clearly sequenced
anchors.5: Complete and clearly sequenced IMRaD structure

dimensions.name: information_preservation
dimensions.description: Is all key information from the draft preserved (research question, method, main finding, implication)?

anchors.1: Key information lost
anchors.3: Most information preserved with minor omissions
anchors.5: All key information preserved and enhanced

dimensions.name: precision
dimensions.description: Are quantitative findings reported with appropriate precision (effect sizes, confidence intervals, sample characteristics)?

anchors.1: Vague qualitative descriptions replace quantitative findings
anchors.5: All key quantitative findings precisely reported

hard_deductions.condition: Introduces new claims not in the draft

dimensions.name: identification_quality
dimensions.description: Is the proposed identification strategy credible and appropriate for the research question?

anchors.1: No identification strategy or naive OLS without discussion
anchors.3: Strategy proposed but with unaddressed endogeneity
anchors.5: Credible strategy with explicit discussion of exogeneity condition

dimensions.name: data_feasibility
dimensions.description: Are the suggested data sources real, accessible, and appropriate?

anchors.1: Data sources are fictional or inaccessible
anchors.5: Specific, named, accessible datasets appropriate to the research question

dimensions.name: threat_completeness
dimensions.description: Are the main threats to validity identified and addressed?

anchors.1: No threats discussed
anchors.5: 3+ major threats identified with specific mitigation strategies

dimensions.name: feasibility_for_researcher
dimensions.description: Is the design realistic given the researcher's stated background and resources?

anchors.1: Design is clearly infeasible given stated constraints
anchors.5: Well-calibrated to researcher's capacity with pragmatic compromises noted

hard_deductions.condition: Proposes an instrument without discussing exclusion restriction
Suggests data that does not exist or is clearly inaccessible
